channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Secret Service fatally shoots suspect outside White House checkpoint, bystander wounded
item.description: The suspect began shooting at posted officers, who returned fire and hit the shooter, the Secret Service said. A bystander was also shot, a law enforcement official said, but it's unclear by whom.
item.content:encoded:
The suspect began shooting at posted officers, who returned fire and hit the shooter, the Secret Service said. A bystander was also shot, a law enforcement official said, but it's unclear by whom.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Romanian director Cristian Mungiu's 'Fjord' wins top prize at Cannes
item.description: Mungiu took home the prestigious Palme D'Or for his film Fjord, a culture-war drama set in Norway.
item.content:encoded:
Fjord — and Sebastian Stan, during the awards ceremony at the 79th Cannes international film festival, in southern France, on Saturday.'/>

Mungiu took home the prestigious Palme D'Or for his film Fjord, a culture-war drama set in Norway.

(Image credit: Andreea Alexandru)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Trump says a deal with Iran and opening of Strait of Hormuz are 'largely negotiated'
item.description: President Trump announced the unfinalized deal on social media after talks with Israel and other allies. It aims to end the war and includes two-month negotiations on Iran's nuclear program.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump announced the unfinalized deal on social media after talks with Israel and other allies. It aims to end the war and includes two-month negotiations on Iran's nuclear program.

(Image credit: Iranian Presidency Office via AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: What will Trump do next with Iran?
item.description: Three months since the start of its conflict with Iran, the Trump administration is oscillating between strike threats and diplomatic overtures. Neither path has yielded a clear resolution.
item.content:encoded:
Three months since the start of its conflict with Iran, the Trump administration is oscillating between strike threats and diplomatic overtures. Neither path has yielded a clear resolution.

(Image credit: Rod Lamkey)
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx

item.title: Kyle Busch died after severe pneumonia progressed into sepsis, his family says
item.description: The two-time NASCAR champion died on Thursday, a day after passing out in a Chevrolet simulator.
item.content:encoded:
The two-time NASCAR champion died on Thursday, a day after passing out in a Chevrolet simulator.

(Image credit: Derik Hamilton)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A little bit country, a little bit Desi: A Pakistani-American's hybrid music
item.description: On May 31, Mo Sabri makes a landmark appearance with the Nashville Symphony for an orchestral rendition of his new album, "Tennessee Desi," which blends Appalachian styles with Muslim devotional music.
item.content:encoded:
On May 31, Mo Sabri makes a landmark appearance with the Nashville Symphony for an orchestral rendition of his new album, "Tennessee Desi," which blends Appalachian styles with Muslim devotional music.

(Image credit: Mo Sabri)
item.dc:creator: Malaka Gharib

item.title: U.S. passengers flying from Ebola-affected countries rerouted
item.description: The U.S. government is responding to the Ebola outbreak in with travel restrictions. American citizens and permanent residents departing affected countries must fly into one of three U.S. airports.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. government is responding to the Ebola outbreak in with travel restrictions. American citizens and permanent residents departing affected countries must fly into one of three U.S. airports.

(Image credit: Michal Ruprecht for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Opinion: Remembering Barney Frank, trailblazing public servant
item.description: Mass. congressman Barney Frank was the first House member to come out as gay and was instrumental in Wall Street reforms after the Great Recession. He died this week at the age of 86.
item.content:encoded:
Mass. congressman Barney Frank was the first House member to come out as gay and was instrumental in Wall Street reforms after the Great Recession. He died this week at the age of 86.

(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Summer electric bills sizzle as the cost of cooling climbs
item.description: The temperature is climbing, and so are people's utility bills. Rising electricity prices and hotter-than-usual weather could make it especially costly to stay cool this summer.
item.content:encoded:
The temperature is climbing, and so are people's utility bills. Rising electricity prices and hotter-than-usual weather could make it especially costly to stay cool this summer.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Chile's MAGA-inspired border control
item.description: Chile digs desert trenches along its northern border as President José Antonio Kast pushes a hardline migration crackdown critics say may have little effect.
item.content:encoded:
Chile digs desert trenches along its northern border as President José Antonio Kast pushes a hardline migration crackdown critics say may have little effect.

(Image credit: John Bartlett)
item.dc:creator: John Bartlett
